text: Being here evokes a sense of suspended anticipation. The first scene is shadowy and sterile, illuminated only by harsh artificial lights reflecting off glass and metal; I feel as though I'm on the threshold of insight or discovery, yet isolated and questioning what lies beyond the barrier. In the second scene, awash with colored light and enigmatic words, I sense my thoughts drifting in the ambiguity—invited to ponder, to be perplexed, to dwell in the comfort of confusion. Here, I am both observer and participant in an experiment of perception and meaning, feeling curiously at home in this blend of uncertainty and wonder.
